text: The Earth orbits the Sun at an average distance of about 93 million miles.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
The capital of France is Paris.
DNA is composed of four nucleotide bases: adenine, thymine, cytosine, and guanine.
The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 meters per second.
Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth above sea level at 8,848 meters.
The first successful powered flight was by the Wright brothers in 1903.
Python was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991.
The human heart has four chambers: two atria and two ventricles.
Shakespeare wrote Romeo and Juliet, believed to be composed between 1591 and 1595.
The periodic table contains 118 confirmed elements.
The Amazon River is the longest river in South America.
Photosynthesis produces oxygen as a byproduct.
The United Nations was founded in 1945 after World War II.
Gravity is described by Newton's law of universal gravitation: F = G(m₁m₂)/r².
The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.
JavaScript was created by Brendan Eich in 1995.
The Great Wall of China is over 13,000 miles long.
Diamonds are composed of carbon atoms arranged in a crystal lattice structure.
The first moon landing occurred on July 20, 1969, with Apollo 11.
ASCII encoding uses 7 bits per character for standard English text.
A hectare is equal to 10,000 square meters.
The freezing point of water is 0 degrees Celsius.
The Library of Congress is the largest library in the world by shelf space.
Canada has ten provinces and three territories.
The mass of a proton is approximately 1.67 × 10⁻²⁷ kilograms.
HTTP stands for HyperText Transfer Protocol.
The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci in the early 1500s.
The Richter scale measures the magnitude of earthquakes.
TCP uses a three-way handshake to establish a connection.
